serviceDescription.aws: AWS CloudTrail is a service that enables governance, compliance, operational auditing, and risk auditing of your AWS account. With CloudTrail, you can log, continuously monitor, and retain account activity related to actions across your AWS infrastructure.
serviceDescription.aws-iso: AWS CloudTrail is a service that enables governance, compliance, operational auditing, and risk auditing of your AWS account. With CloudTrail, you can log, continuously monitor, and retain account activity related to actions across your AWS infrastructure.
serviceDescription.aws-iso-b: AWS CloudTrail is a service that enables governance, compliance, operational auditing, and risk auditing of your AWS account. With CloudTrail, you can log, continuously monitor, and retain account activity related to actions across your AWS infrastructure.
serviceDescription.aws-cn: Amazon CloudTrail is a service that enables governance, compliance, operational auditing, and risk auditing of your Amazon account. With CloudTrail, you can log, continuously monitor, and retain account activity related to actions across your Amazon infrastructure.
serviceDescription.aws-us-gov: AWS CloudTrail is a service that enables governance, compliance, operational auditing, and risk auditing of your AWS account. With CloudTrail, you can log, continuously monitor, and retain account activity related to actions across your AWS infrastructure.

cards.title: Management Events
cards.helpText: Management events capture control plane actions performed on a resource, such as creating or deleting Amazon S3 buckets.

components.text: The first copy of management events within each region is delivered free of charge.

components.label: Management events units
components.description: Select the units for read and write management events based on expected volume (millions is most common).

components.label: Write management events
components.placeholder: Enter number of write management events in units selected
components.description: Management events for API calls such as creation, deletion, and modification of Amazon EC2 instances or  Amazon S3 buckets.

components.label: Write management trails
components.description: Number of trails delivering write management events (0-5).

components.label: Read management events
components.placeholder: Enter number of read management events in units selected
components.description: Management events for API calls that read resources but don't make changes.

components.label: Read management trails
components.description: Number of trails delivering read management events (0-5).

components.label: Price of management events per month

operands.variableLabel: Price of management events per month
Price of management events per month

cards.title: Data Events
cards.helpText: Data events capture high-volume data plane actions performed on or within a resource, such as reading or writing an Amazon S3 object.

components.text: Data events are recorded only for the Lambda functions and S3 buckets you specify.

components.label: Data events units
components.description: Select the units for S3 and Lambda operations based on expected volume (millions is most common).

components.label: S3 operations
components.placeholder: Enter number of S3 operations in units selected
components.description: Amazon S3 object level APIs such as GetObject, PutObject, DeleteObject and List actions. Typically customer have around 2 million operations each month per S3 bucket.

components.label: S3 trails
components.description: Number of trails delivering S3 operations (0-5).

components.label: Lambda data events
components.placeholder: Enter number of Lambda operations in units selected

components.label: Lambda trails
components.description: Number of trails delivering Lambda operations (0-5).

components.label: Price for data events per month

operands.variableLabel: Price for data events per month
Price for data events per month

helpText.aws: AWS CloudTrail Insights analyzes write management event API calls in your AWS account and detects unusual activity.
helpText.aws-iso: AWS CloudTrail Insights analyzes write management event API calls in your AWS account and detects unusual activity.
helpText.aws-iso-b: AWS CloudTrail Insights analyzes write management event API calls in your AWS account and detects unusual activity.
helpText.aws-cn: Amazon CloudTrail Insights analyzes write management event API calls in your Amazon account and detects unusual activity.
helpText.aws-us-gov: AWS CloudTrail Insights analyzes write management event API calls in your AWS account and detects unusual activity.

text.aws: CloudTrail Insights provides visibility into unusual operational activity in your AWS account by analyzing the API call volume of write management events.
text.aws-iso: CloudTrail Insights provides visibility into unusual operational activity in your AWS account by analyzing the API call volume of write management events.
text.aws-iso-b: CloudTrail Insights provides visibility into unusual operational activity in your AWS account by analyzing the API call volume of write management events.
text.aws-cn: CloudTrail Insights provides visibility into unusual operational activity in your Amazon account by analyzing the API call volume of write management events.

components.label: Insight events units
components.description: Select the units for write managment events based on expected volume (millions is most common).

components.label: Number of write management events analyzed
components.placeholder: Enter number of write managmement events in units selected
components.description: If CloudTrail Insights detects unusual activity, it delivers an Insights event to you.

components.label: Trails with Insight events
components.description: Number of trails with Insight events.

operands.variableLabel: Number of write management events analyzed

cards.title: CloudTrail Lake
cards.helpText: For CloudTrail Lake, you pay for data ingestion, retention, and analysis. Ingestion charges are based on the volume and type of data ingested to your event data store(s). Retention charges are based on the selected pricing option and the volume of data retained within your event data store(s). Data querying, performed by CloudTrail Lake, charges are based on the amount of data scanned by your queries. The same data querying charges apply when you run a SQL query created with the natural language query generation (in preview) feature. CloudTrail Lake offers two pricing options: (1) one-year extendable retention pricing and (2) seven-year retention pricing.

components.label: Data ingested - CloudTrail
components.description: One-year extendable retention pricing for CloudTrail management and data events

description.aws: One-year extendable retention pricing for other AWS and non-AWS source
description.aws-iso: One-year extendable retention pricing for other AWS and non-AWSsource
description.aws-iso-b: One-year extendable retention pricing for other AWS and non-AWS source
description.aws-iso-e: One-year extendable retention pricing for other AWS and non-AWS source
description.aws-iso-f: One-year extendable retention pricing for other AWS and non-AWS source
description.aws-cn: One-year extendable retention pricing for other Amazon and non-Amazon source

components.label: Data Retention - One-year extendable retention pricing
components.description: Volume of data you retain in CloudTrail Lake one year after the ingestion of this data. There are no retention charges to store the data for the first year after ingestion. Please note, this option is not relevant for data ingested in the 7-year retention pricing.

components.label: Price for ingestion other data sources

components.outputUnitLabel: [currency] Data ingested for CloudTrail management and data events

operands.variableLabel: Price for ingestion other data sources

components.outputUnitLabel: [currency] Data ingested for other data sources

operands.variableUnitLabel: [currency] (Data ingested for CloudTrail management and data events)
